title: Openness of Executive Recruitment Score
subtitle: Data by [Polity 5](#dod:polity).
caption:
Openness of Executive Recruitment Score, 2020

Data by Polity 5.

Across the globe in 2020, the method of executive recruitment varies significantly, as shown in this choropleth map using a continuous color scale. The scale ranges from dark red, representing "Power seized" (numeric value 0), through orange and purple bands for "Hereditary succession" and "Dual, chief minister designated/elected," to dark blue, representing "Open" recruitment (numeric value 4). The figures exclude international aviation and other external factors not captured by the Polity 5 dataset. The most widespread category is "Open," covering the vast majority of the Americas, including the United States, Canada, and Brazil, as well as nearly all of Europe, Australia, and parts of southern Africa like South Africa. In contrast, the "Power seized" category, indicated by the darkest red, is visible in specific regions including North Africa (Libya, Algeria), the Middle East (Yemen, Syria), and parts of Southeast Asia (Myanmar). Countries falling into the intermediate "Hereditary succession" band, shown in orange, include Saudi Arabia and Oman on the Arabian Peninsula. A few nations, such as Russia and Thailand, appear in the lighter red or purple bands, indicating systems where the chief minister is designated or elected through dual processes rather than fully open competition or outright seizure. Greenland and a small portion of the Sahara are marked with hatching to indicate no data is available for those territories. The dominant visible pattern is a global concentration of open executive recruitment in the Western Hemisphere and Western Europe, contrasted with clusters of seized power and hereditary systems concentrated in North Africa, the Middle East, and parts of Asia.
